finding_results.notes: Reworked the stairwell assent sequence so zero speakers besides Noor deliver a full self-contained explanation of their file. Samir now registers consent through gesture only (thumb on bracket edge, nod, no speech). Helen's action stands alone without narrator commentary explaining its meaning. James's line breaks mid-phrase ('My daughter's —') into incomplete speech before he recovers with a bare 'Yeah. Go.' Rachel's pragmatic 'We going?' remains. Viktor still polls Samir by name (character-true procedural habit) but receives physical assent rather than a summary. Four of five non-Noor speakers now register through action, incomplete speech, or pragmatic urgency rather than polished mini-confessions.

summary: Addressed the single finding by breaking the round-robin consent-collection cadence in the stairwell scene. Samir's spoken dossier summary replaced with silent gestural assent. James's compact file-description replaced with a broken-off fragment. Helen's narrator-explained silence reduced to bare action. The group's acceptance of the dead man's switch still reads clearly through physical and incomplete-verbal cues, but the exchange no longer cycles through polished personal statements.
